observations.recommendation: We commended the MLGU headed by the Local Chief Executive (LCE) for its worthy accomplishments and performance, and encourage them to continue such exemplary practices, not only for purposes of receiving awards, recognitions and citations, but more importantly, for the betterment of the living condition of its constituents through good governance.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (MAcco) prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to accurately reflect the correct balance of the Due to NGAs account, as well as the Due to GOCCs and Trust Liabilities accounts. Additionally, we recommended that the MAcco ensure adherence to the proper classification of accounts, in strict compliance with Section 4 (7) of PD No. 1445 and the RCA for LGUs.

We recommended the following to the MAcco:
Adjust the books with the guidance of the following entry:
Other General Services		₱19,970,734.13
Representation Expenses		    3,661,028.50
Prizes			                   212,700.00
Donations			         98,400.00
Gas, Oil & Lubricants		         10,694.64
OMOE			                   ₱23,953,557.27;
Prepare the Schedule of OMOE account from CDJ and those credited to Accounts Payable account, review the charges to OMOE, reclassify those items which are classifiable under specific expense account; and
Ensure that expenses are charged in their appropriate accounts and avoid the instance of charging items to OMOE account when the same can be specifically categorized under specific expense accounts.

We recommended that the MAcco ensure proper recording of liability accounts based on their nature and purpose in accordance with LGCOA, and adjust the books with the guidance of the following compound entry:
GF books:
Trust Liabilities					8,708,204.15
Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable			6,073,713.73
Due to NGAs					1,009,094.66
Due to GOCCs					   567,819.00
Due to LGUs					     24,000.00
Deferred Real Property Tax			1,033,576.76
TF Books
Trust Liabilities					30,870,575.17
Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable			 1,591,743.45
Due to NGAs					          27,691,066.18
Due to LGUs					    1,587,765.54

We recommended that the MAcco:
Make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances that were misstated as at year-end; and
Drop from the books the seven units of rescue vehicles already donated to the Barangays of Bagumbayan, Palabotan, Kinabuan, Domang, Lukidnon, Dopaj and Balzain, all in Dupax del Sur, and the one unit of fire truck donated to BFP and attach the Deeds of Donation as basis in removing the accounts, copy furnish the Audit Team.

We recommended that the MAcco make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct amount of Advances to Contractors and other affected accounts and ensure compliance with the proper accounting of mobilization fee as prescribed under the RCA for LGUs to achieve fairly presented FS as required by Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the MAcco:
Adjust the books in accordance with the proposed adjusting entries discussed in the audit of Trust Liabilities account; and
Review the Schedule of Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable account and investigate the cause/s of the variance noted in the schedule and FS balance and adjust accordingly to the reconciliation statement.

We recommended the following:
The Inventory Committee and the MAcco to provide a reconciliation of noted variance in the Other Structures account and the latter to adjust the books for such variance; and
The MAcco to reclassify the error to its appropriate PPE account.

We recommended that the MAcco prepare adjusting journal entries to correct the misclassification of accounts particularly the Due from LGUs, Due from NGOs/POs, and OMOE accounts totaling ₱785,565.00 in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 to come up with a more accurate and reliable financial information.

We recommended that the MAcco prepare adjusting journal entries to correct the misclassification of accounts in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 to come up with a more accurate and reliable financial information.

We recommended that the MAcco make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balance of the affected accounts, Accumulated Depreciation - Flood Control Systems and Depreciation - Infrastructure Assets, and henceforth, strictly observe the policies on the recognition of completed projects and depreciation of PPE.
Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Engineer (ME) and MAcco reconcile the completions dates reported in the Status of Implementation of Projects for the Year 2024 with the Lapsing and/or Depreciation Schedule and make the necessary adjustments, then submit a copy of the reconciled reports to the Audit Team.
Also, we recommended to the MAcco that completed projects be immediately reclassified to the appropriate asset accounts and the related depreciation be taken up in the books upon commencement of operation or usage.
Furthermore, we recommended that the GSO and/or Inventory Committee verify and validate the items in the Flood Control System account as recorded in the accounting reports but not reported in the RPCPPE and adjust the property reports accordingly.

We recommended the following:
The MAcco to adjust the books with the guidance of the following entry:
Repairs and Maintenance-Motor Vehicles		 ₱10,896.34
Other Supplies and Materials for Distribution	         ₱10,896.34; and
The Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of inventory of supplies and materials at year-end with reconciliation of any noted differences with the books of the MAcco for the latter’s adjustments in the book.

We recommended to the MAcco the following:
Re-group aged 91 to 365 days accounts into 91 to 180 days and 181 to 365 days and reclassify those accounts that have been outstanding for 181 to 365 days, and adjust the books accordingly, with the guidance of the following entries:
CIB-LCCA				xxx
Accounts Payable			xxx;
Contact the holders of stale check and replace them with new ones and record the replacement with the following entries:
Accounts Payable			xxx
CIB-LCCA			xxx

We recommended that the MAcco reconcile the difference in the utilized portion of details of the unexpended balance of DRRMF as presented in Report on Sources and Utilization of DDRMF and its related disclosure in the Notes to FS and furnish the Audit Team of proofs of reconciliation.

We recommended that the MAcco make the necessary restatement of the affected accounts in the FS and recognize the prior period errors at the earliest period presented in the comparative FS and properly identify prior period errors and provide adequate disclosure to properly account for the same.

We recommended the following:
The MA to prepare and submit to the LCE a narrative report on the nature, facts and circumstances about these breeding animals which will be used as basis in the derecognition of their recorded values in the books of the Municipality; and
The Management to request authority for the derecognition of the booked-up and accounted as breeding animals considering that these are not considered among the property of the Municipality but intended for dispersal to identified individuals/farmers by DA and considering that the MLGU’s only role is to distribute them but has no control over said animals.

We recommended that the MAcco implement the necessary controls to maintain accurate and updated SLC for each commodity or stock. In addition, we recommended the GSO to ensure that SC are kept and up to date to reflect inventory receipts and issuances and to conduct periodic physical inventory counts to be reported in the RPCI and submitted to the Audit Team within the prescribed deadlines of July 31 and January 31 of each year.

We commended the Management for the continuous effort in collecting long-outstanding advances from officers and employees still connected with the agency and encourage them to extend these efforts even with individuals who are no longer employed by the agency.
Even with the notable amount of collections, we still recommended the MAcco, in addition to issuing demand letters, to further look into the status of the availability of the records or documents through the AO responsible for handling the account as indicated in Item 7.1.f of COA Circular No. 2023-008 and be able to indicate the extent of validation made and prepare a detailed list of the lost documents. The result of which will be the initial step in the conduct of an investigation to be conducted by a duly created Investigation Committee.
We also reiterated our previous year’s recommendation to Management that if all appropriate remedies to recover overdue receivables were already made, to comply with the set guidelines outlined in COA Circular No. 2023-008 on the one-time cleansing of dormant accounts, taking into consideration the timeframe prescribed thereto.

We recommended that the MAcco ensure that its disclosure fully comply with the provisions of Paragraph 129 of IPSAS 1 and Paragraph 36 of IPSAS 19, and explicitly identify whether any contingent liabilities exist in the Notes to FS.

We recommended that the MAcco observe the disclosure requirements in the preparation of the FS in accordance with IPSAS Nos. 1 and 20 to ensure that the entity is transparent about its dealings with related parties and provide users with additional information in understanding the FS and aid in the decision-making as well as achieve fair presentation of the FS.

We recommended Management to:
Give priority to existing COS workers in the appointment by the MLGU to its vacant regular positions provided that these workers meet the appropriate eligibility and other qualification requirements for the position subject to existing Civil Service law and rules; and
Ensure that hiring of COS are based on necessity of the Office/Department to avoid waste of government funds and must conform with the pertinent provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR.

We recommended the following:
The Local Sanggunian, with the assistance of the Local Finance Committee (LFC), to conduct a more detailed analysis of accounts Fuel, Oil & Lubricants Expense, Repairs and Maintenance -Land Transportation Equipment, Other Personnel Benefit and Other Bonuses and Allowances to determine if the amounts allocated are proportionate to their actual impact and necessity;
The MBO, MAcco and MT to ensure that proper budgeting, accounting, and collections of receipts and expenditures are correctly accounted for per office, per activity and or per object of expenditures; and
The LCE to strengthen internal controls to ensure that expenses do not exceed the approved budget and that expenses are only incurred when there is a clear source of cash support.

We recommended that the MAcco resolve the above-mentioned reconciling items and identify the cause of discrepancies as well as locate all available data and supporting documents of the unreconciled items in the SLs to come up with a more accurate and reliable information.
Further, if once reconciled and resulted to unremitted premiums, we recommended that the MT observe the prescribed due dates for the remittances of premium contributions and loans in compliance to the aforementioned GSIS and Pag-IBIG laws, rules and regulations in order to avoid incurrence of possible penalties.

We recommended that the ME and MAcco reconcile the Schedule of the CIP account with the Status of Implementation of 20% DF (INFRA) for the year 2024 and make the necessary adjustments, then submit a copy of the reconciled reports to the Audit Team.
Once reconciled, we recommended that the MAcco make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balance of the affected accounts, CIP, Buildings and Other Structures and Depreciation accounts, if any.

We commended Management with its compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 with respect to its recording of completed projects in the books of accounts. We look forward to the same compliance in the future.
Moreover, with respect to charging expenses against the DRRM Fund, we recommended Management to refrain from charging wages of JO against the DRRMF and fully comply with NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JC 2013-01 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1. Instead, the amount should be charged against the regular fund appropriated for MOOE in the Office of the MDDRMO.

We recommended the following:
The Gender Focal Point Systems to utilize HGDG tool as an assessment mechanism for a more substantiated accomplishment report and that the information contained therein is justified by evidence relevant for decision-making and budgeting. If trainings are necessary on the use of the tool, it may include said training in its GPB and may seek the assistance of an expert on gender analysis using the HGDG; and
Management as a whole to integrate in the budget possible response to the weaknesses identified by the HGDG tool to comply with the requirement of the MLGU to be gender sensitive and gender responsive.

We recommended Management to promptly issue the Certificates of Final Acceptance for all projects where the DLP has already expired and after all defects and failures, if any, shall have been repaired by the contractor to the satisfaction of the Project Engineer (a Final Acceptance Inspection Report must be rendered by the Inspectorate Team for the matter), and require the contractors to post warranty security in the form prescribed in Section 62.2.3.3 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184 (Section 90.2.3.c. of the IRR of RA No. 12009). Henceforward, we recommended the ME to implement a tracking system to monitor the expiration of the DLP for each completed project to guarantee the timely issuance of CFA and to activate thereof the warranty period.

We recommended that the BAC ensure submission of printout copies of Invitation to Bid posted in the PhilGEPS reflecting its status and the certification of the BAC Secretariat that the Invitation to Bid has been posted in conspicuous places and the certification of the BAC Secretariat that the Invitation to Bid has been posted in conspicuous places and include the Certification in the procurement records submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the BAC ensure the strict use of the RFQ form and other procurement forms prescribed by the GPPB to enhance the transparency, accountability, and the legitimacy of the procurement process.

We recommended that the LCE, as the Chairperson together with other co-chairpersons and members of the School Board to:
Convene and inform among its members the required indicative timeline as set forth in Item 6.1, DEPED-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017 and strictly comply with the deadline set forth in the said guidelines;
Coordinate with DepEd SDS for the latter to timely submit the prior year’s required reports which shall be the basis in preparing the SEF budget to avoid delays in the timeline of SEF budgeting; and
Furnishing of copies of SEF Budget to MBO, MAcco and MT pursuant to prescribed indicative timeline as well as consistently monitor the transmittal of the SEF budget and ensure that MAcco and MT are furnished with a copy of the thereof.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to the LSB:
Inform all members of the LSB including the MAcco on the required submission of SEF utilization report to DepEd with copy furnished to SB, DBM RO and DILG RO;
Instruct the MAcco to prepare the annual utilization report of the SEF; and
Ensure submission, through the LSB Secretariat, of the SEF quarterly and annual utilization report to the DepEd CO by its transmittal to DepEd Nueva Vizcaya Division and furnish the same to SB, DBM RO and DILG RO within the period set forth by DEPED-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended to the LCE, as Chairperson of the LSB, in coordination with the other co-chairpersons and members of the LSB, to fully comply with the required posting of all the Quarterly SEF Utilization Reports on the website of the MLGU and/or in at least three conspicuous public places and to continue with the regular posting in the FDPP for transparency and accountability in compliance with the Full Disclosure Policy of the DILG as indicated in the above-mentioned circulars.

We recommended Management to compute and impose the liquidated damages against the Contractor and furnish proof of collection supported by report of accomplishment as of expiry date, provided that no justification is submitted on non-imposition of LD.

We recommended the MAcco, the MT and the Inventory Committee to immediately reconcile their records and effect the necessary adjustments on the affected accounts in compliance with pertinent provisions of the NGAS Manual for LGUs. Provide to the Office of the Auditor proofs of reconciliation undertaken.
We recommended the following:
The MAcco and General Services Officer (GSO) to reconcile the Land account per books and per records and trace the existing CT with the recorded ones for accurate valuation of Land account. After which, the MAcco record the existing CT not recorded in the books pursuant to IPSAS;
The Management with respect to unregistered parcels of land to prioritize the registration and transfer of the title of land in the name of LGU Dupax del Sur to have conclusive evidence of ownership and to fully protect the interest of the Municipality in adherence to Section 39 (2) of PD No. 1445 and Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20 1992.
In addition to that, with respect to parcels of land casted with clouds:
Furnish the auditor with a copy of TCT No. 90898 to ensure that the same was not lost;
Initiate the transfer of TCT No. 143416 from the names of Spouses A to the name of LGU of Dupax del Sur; and
Tract an update of the case regarding the settlement of estate of deceased, and if already settled, initiate the removal of the annotation or memorandum of encumbrances on the said title.
We recommended the MAcco to reclassify the errors to their appropriate PPE accounts as guided by the following:

We recommended that Management fully implement the projects that have been debited to the CIP accounts for a relatively long period of time for them to be reclassified to their appropriate accounts and subjected to depreciation to properly effect the buildings’ and infrastructure assets’ wear and tear to attain a fairly valued FSs pursuant to Paragraph 14 of IPSAS 17 and COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015. Further, we recommended that Management through the ME and the MAcco to henceforth install procedures to effectively monitor and promptly report projects undertaken and include in the Status of Implementation Report all projects which are yet to be implemented including those pertaining to the prior years.

We recommended the MAcco to ensure that the provision of the depreciation expense beginning from the applicable month after the PPE items are reclassified from CIP accounts to their corresponding assets accounts and consider adjusting the books with the following proposed adjusting entries.
Should be entry:
Depreciation Expense-Infrastructure Asset	₱445,418.75
Depreciation Expense-Buildings		    55,232.98
Accumulated Depreciation- Infrastructure Asset 		₱445,418.75
Accumulated Depreciation-Buildings		     55,232.98
To adjust the permanent errors and recognize the unrecorded depreciation
Entry made:
No entries
Adjusting Entry since the books are already closed:
Prior Years’ Adjustments			₱500,651.72
Accumulated Depreciation- Infrastructure Asset 		₱445,418.75
Accumulated Depreciation-Buildings		     55,232.98
To correct the permanent errors and recognize the unrecorded depreciation.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the MAcco to make proper disclosures of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF under the account TL-DRRMF in the Notes to FS in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002;
Enjoin the concerned officials to strictly comply with the reportorial requirements laid out in COA Circulars 2012-002 and 2014-002 to have a more reliable and accurate TL-DRRMF account and proper monitoring report for 5% LDRRM Fund utilization and balances; and
Strictly and prudently comply with all the rules and regulations laid out relative to LDRRM Fund.

We recommended the MT to immediately transfer the cash back-up of the unexpended balances of 5% LDRRM Fund (QRF and MF-MOOE) from the GF account to the TF account in order to fairly present the CIB-LCCA account and undertake the same in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. Likewise, we recommended the MAcco to comply with the prescribed accounting entries for the unexpended QRF and MF-MOOE from Current Appropriation of the 5% LDRRM Fund.

We recommended the MAcco to reconcile the “Due from Other Funds” and “Due to Other Funds” accounts and make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances and submit proofs of the same to the Office of the Auditor. Likewise, we recommended her to undertake regular and timely review and reconciliation of the two (2) reciprocal accounts in compliance to the above-mentioned rules and regulations.

We recommended the MAcco to reconcile the noted difference of ₱2,344,408.84 existing on the two (2) receivable accounts and furnish results of reconciliation or adjusting entries made to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended the MAcco to resolve the above-mentioned reconciling items and identify the cause of discrepancies in the Subsidiary Ledgers to come up with a more accurate and reliable information.
Likewise, we recommended that Management identify the cause(s) of the deficiency and coordinate immediately with the GSIS to reconcile and update agency/employee records.
Further, if once reconciled and resulted to unremitted premiums, we recommended the MT to observe the prescribed due dates for the remittances of withheld taxes, premium contributions and loans in compliance to the aforementioned BIR, Pag-IBIG, PhilHealth and GSIS laws, rules and regulations in order to avoid incurrence of possible penalties.

We recommended the LCE and Local Sanggunian to formulate clear-cut guidelines on allocations and granting of cell cards and furnish the same guidelines to the Office of the Auditor for reference in audit.
Likewise, we recommended the MAcco to make certain that used cards are surrendered prior to processing and payment of cell card allowance to guarantee proper use of government resources.

We recommended Management to furnish to the Office of the Auditor its legal basis which authorizes it to disburse the limited funds of the government for SEI to its JOs. Absence of which, the total disbursements of ₱1,439,600.00 can be considered an irregular expenditure, hence, disallowable in audit consistent with COA Circular No. 2012-003.

We recommended Management to submit a report on the extraordinary suggestion, invention, superior accomplishment and other personal effects of each individual who received the PRAISE Incentive Bonus including the computation of the savings generated or realized therefrom in which the Municipality has been benefited.

We recommended that Management cause the immediate refund of the rice allowance and medical allowance paid to its municipal officials, regular and casual employees and job orders in contrary to RA No. 6758. Likewise, we recommended that Management only grant allowances and benefits authorized under the Act, subject to funds availability. Further, such other additional compensation not otherwise specified in RA No. 6758 may be granted as may be determined by the Department of Budget and Management.

We recommended that the LCE, in collaboration with the MSWDO and the SB, establish a clear-cut policy/guidelines and procedures for the grants and donations. This should be done with thorough consideration of the social and living conditions of recipients to ensure a fair, just, and equitable distribution of funds.
In addition, we recommended that Management provide its written justification on the legality and reasonableness of the expenditures totaling ₱31,800.00. Also, we recommended that Management stop granting financial assistance intended for private purposes, or it shall result to these expenditures subject to audit disallowances.

We recommended that Management, through the LCE, enforce the immediate refund of the excessive RATA paid to the Municipal Vice Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan Members, otherwise ND shall be issued pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-003.

We recommended Management through the MAcco to comply with the set guidelines outlined in COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023 on the one-time cleansing of dormant accounts taking into consideration the timeframe prescribed thereto.

We recommended that Management enforce its computed LD against the defaulting Contractor of the project “Installation of Water System at Dupax del Sur Fire Station” and submit to the Office of the Auditor proof of refund for validation. Likewise, we recommended that Management demand installation of the additional steel matting work worth ₱16,688.75 for similar project.
Further, we recommended that Management provide its written explanation on the issuance of undated Certificate of Completion specifying that the project “Construction of Multi-Purpose Hall (Nutrition Building) at Barangay Gabut” was satisfactorily completed on February 28, 2023 ahead of the SWA (100%) dated March 03, 2023, for evaluation in audit. We also recommended Management to implement controls to ensure that Certificates of Completion are issued only after a comprehensive assessment of work accomplished has been conducted, aligning closely with project timelines and milestones.

We recommended that all Officials concerned who use or whose Offices are with assigned government motor vehicles cease from collecting the TA, failure to do so will subject the same disbursements to audit disallowance since it is contrary to Item 8.2.1 of LBC No. 103. Likewise, we recommended Management to stop from paying RATA in advance pursuant to Section 88 (1) of PD No. 1445.
Further, we recommended that Management (1) enjoin the municipal officials and heads of offices to specify the (a) type of leave applied for in a particular day on their Daily Time Records and (b) mode of transportation on their Travel Orders to facilitate the pre-audit by the MAcco on the propriety and validity of their future RATA claims, and (2) require them to submit a complete set of Daily Time Records (DTRs), approved leave applications, and approved Travel Orders to substantiate the RATA disbursed in year 2023.

We recommended the following:
The MDRRM Officer to provide a written justification as to the improper utilization of the 30% QRF considering the provincial declaration and release of the financial assistance; and
Management, through the MDRRM Officer, to observe the provisions of Section 21 of RA No. 10121 or Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act of 2010 and Joint MC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the prescribed period of payment of Uniform or Clothing Allowance which is the month of April as explicitly stated in the aforesaid DBM issuances in order to avoid future audit disallowances.

We recommended that Management provide a written justification as to the improper utilization of the 70% MF for evaluation in audit and strictly and prudently comply with all the rules and regulations laid out relative to the proper utilization of LDRRM Fund.

We recommended Management to strictly abide by the reportorial requirements, procedures and guidelines set forth in COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND JC No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 to fully strengthen peace and order in the community that will benefit its constituents.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to coordinate with the League of Municipalities of Nueva Vizcaya and demand for the issuance of government prescribed official receipts (Accountable Form No.51) for all disbursements to the League of Municipalities of the Philippines (LMP)-Nueva Vizcaya Chapter to fully establish financial accountability and to protect public funds to proper use.

We recommended Management to provide to the Office of the Auditor a written explanation why up to now these projects were not yet completed. Likewise, we recommended the ME to compute for LD in view of possible delays in the implementation of the projects to be deducted from their billings for payments, and submit it together with pertinent documents and all the SWA as of expiry date of these projects. Further, we recommended Management to impose such computed LD and other sanctions provided in the IRR of RA 9184 to the erring contractors and submit proof of actions taken to the Office of the Auditor for monitoring.
In addition, we recommended Management the following:
Conduct a thorough review of existing project schedules to identify bottlenecks and critical path activities contributing to possible delays;
Implement stronger monitoring protocols to track project progress effectively, enabling timely intervention to address potential delays and deviations from the planned timeline; and
Ensure strict adherence to contractual obligations, including timelines, to mitigate the risk of LD and legal repercussions.

We recommended the LCE to direct the AO to liquidate her cash advances within the prescribed period and ensure that no additional cash advance shall be allowed unless the previous ones are settled, properly accounted and documented. Lastly, the Disbursing Officer and other concerned officials and employees to strictly comply with the laws, rules and regulations cited above on the grant and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended to Management that the appropriated amounts of the 20% DF be fully utilized for the implementation and accomplishments of the lined-up projects contained in the approved Annual Investment Plan in order to optimally attain its desired benefits to the advantage of its constituents.

We recommended the following:
As a withholding agent, Management to ensure that taxes be withheld on payment of BAC honoraria and promptly remit to the Bureau of Internal Revenue said taxes withheld within the prescribed period to avoid penalties, surcharges and interests which if in any case of late remittance, it shall be personally borne by the defaulting LGU officials concerned;
The BAC to submit the required supporting documents enumerated in the COA Circular for the disbursements of BAC honoraria made in the year 2023 for further evaluation; and
The MAcco to ascertain the completeness of the documents required by COA Circular No. 2012-001 prior to processing of payment of BAC honoraria to avoid audit suspension or disallowance, if warranted.

We recommended that the Gender Focal Point System Officer ensure the annual preparation of the GAD Plan using the format in Annex N of this report which will be the basis of the GAD budget in the Local Expenditure Program and submit the same to the office of the auditor. Further, the GAD Plan to include the element of Institutionalizing GAD Database/Sex-disaggregated Data which could show a holistic picture of the organization with respect to implementation of a gender- responsive institution. This could be done through survey as data-gathering tools and summarized for basis of GAD plan preparation.
We recommended that the Gender Focal Point System Officer ensure compliance with the required format of the GAD Accomplishment Report as presented in Annex B of PCW-NEDA-DBM JC No. 2012-01 and prepare the necessary attachments for complete support of such accomplishments.

We recommended that Management adhere to the funding requirement of one percent of its IRA to support its LCPC, as mandated by Section 15 of RA No. 9344. Upon allocation, it is essential to guarantee the effective implementation of priority plans for children, such as those outlined in Item II of DILG MC No. 2012-150.

We recommended that the AO reconcile his cashbook with the accounting ledgers at least quarterly. Discrepancies identified should be adjusted accordingly to come up with updated, accurate and reliable balance of accountabilities.
We recommended that the concerned employee prepare the CRAAF to properly establish the accountability of the collectors and avoid misappropriation of collections.
We recommended that LO update his insufficient fidelity bond coverage to an amount ideally equal to his average monthly collection to cover-up possible risk of loss of government money.

We recommended that the AO reconcile her cashbook with the accounting ledgers at least quarterly. Discrepancies identified should be adjusted accordingly to come up with updated, accurate and reliable balance of accountabilities.
We recommended that the concerned employee prepare the RAAF without money value for checks to properly establish the accountability of the cash advances and avoid misappropriation of government moneys.

We recommended Management through the MT to increase the fidelity bond of the Disbursing Officer in compliance with Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02-2019 dated April 25, 2019 to protect government funds.

We recommended Management to enjoin the BAC and other concerned personnel to furnish the Office of the Auditor copies of government contracts and purchase orders not later than five (5) working days from perfection thereof, in line with COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended the MAcco to require submission of complete supporting documents prior to processing of payments to contractors or suppliers. Likewise, we recommended that all Implementing Offices strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2012-001 in order to prevent audit suspensions or disallowances.

We recommended the Municipal Vice Mayor-Presiding Officer and the SB Members to provide the Office of the Auditor with a written explanation addressing the identified lapses in the conduct of regular sessions for further assessment in audit. Additionally, we recommended strict adherence to the provisions of the LGC and other related issuances.

We recommended the LGU-Employer to limit its counterpart contribution amounting to ₱200.00 per month per official or employee effective February 2024 per Pag-IBIG Circular No. 460 unless the requisite DBM consultation and approval is secured, otherwise, Management must submit its legal basis which exempts it from said consultation and approval to avoid audit disallowances.

We recommended that the Accountant reclassify the total cost of completed infrastructure project into the proper infrastructure asset account in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 and recognize Accounts Payable for unbilled portion of the completed project in line with Section 4, Chapter 2, Volume I of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for LGUs and pertinent provisions of IPSAS 17.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review and provide the complete information required in the PPE Schedule and prepare the necessary Adjusting Journal Entry (AJE) to correct the balance of the appropriate Accumulated Depreciation and Government Equity accounts reported in the financial statements, in line with Section 27 of IPSAS 1 and Section 111 of P.D. 1445.
We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Accountant, prepare adjusting journal entries to correct the misclassification of account, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.
We recommended that Management require the Inventory Committee to rectify the submitted RPCPPE by providing all the information required therein. Moreover, we recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant, the General Services Officer and the Inventory Committee to immediately reconcile their records and effect the necessary adjustments on the affected accounts in compliance with pertinent provisions of the NGAS for LGUs.
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to prepare and update the PPELCs and PCs in accordance with pertinent provisions of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Agriculturist to:
submit the inventory of breeding stocks and other biological assets, and provide information on the prevailing market price for each item as of reporting date which shall be reflected in the financial statements; and
conduct inspection to locate and know the present condition and stewardship of these animals, plant and trees and furnish the Audit Team of the report thereon.
We recommended that Management:
direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count and submit the required Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System;
require the Municipal Accountant to recognize the Local Road Networks previously maintained in the Registry of Public Infrastructures into the books of accounts of the LGU and disclose the necessary information in the Notes to Financial Statements pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015. Moreover, exert efforts to retrieve information on projects implemented prior to CY 2011 for recording and monitoring;
direct the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
instruct the Municipal Engineer to provide the local accountant and the general services officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

We recommended that Management require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of the Inventories and henceforth submit the required RPCI to the Audit Team within the prescribed deadline in strict adherence with Section 124, Chapter 7, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GSO to prepare and update the SLCs and SCs in accordance with pertinent provisions of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
We recommended that Management require the Municipal General Services Officer to summarize weekly all issuances of materials and inventory in the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and immediately submit to the Municipal Accountant as basis for the appropriate recording of expenses.

We recommended the Management to strictly observe the appropriate use of the chart of accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009 to facilitate proper accounting and reporting of LGU transactions.

We recommended that Management through the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary Adjusting Journal Entry (AJE) to correct the balance of Advances to Contractors and other related accounts reported in the financial statements in line with Section 27 of IPSAS 1 and Section 111 of P.D. 1445.

We recommended that Management ensure the full implementation of projects and cause the prompt liquidation and refund of unexpended balances of Due to NGAs and Trust Liabilities to the source agency/individual to fully settle the obligations. When necessary, request from the latter an authority to use unspent funds for other related projects, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary journal entries to adjust all reconciling items/discrepancies noted herein. Moreover, we recommended the regular adjustment of reconciling items identified to accurately present the LGU’s actual cash position for each period.

We recommended that Management through the Municipal Accountant immediately review the subsidiary schedules, analyze the recording of transactions which may have caused the negative balances in the subsidiary schedules, and effect the necessary adjustments to the affected accounts in line with Section 27 of IPSAS 1 and Section 111 of P.D. 1445.

We recommended that Management, in addition with employing intensive campaign efforts, coordinate with the Provincial Treasury Office (PTO) and the Department of Environment and Natural Resources (DENR) to:
clarify the validity of the levied Real Property Taxes, particularly those areas under forest zone and Certificate of Ancestral Domain Claim (CADC) / Certificate of Ancestral Domain Title (CADT); and
exhaust all possible legal remedies available to encourage payment from delinquent taxpayers, where applicable.

We commended the Management for its accomplishment in the liquidation of outstanding cash advances that resulted to significant decrease thereof, and encouraged them to continue to strictly implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445 on the proper granting and liquidation of cash advances to avoid the accumulation of long outstanding balances. The “No liquidation, no additional cash advance” policy must be strictly implemented.
Moreover, for the remaining unliquidated cash advances, we recommended the constant monitoring and issuance of demand letters to officials and employees, particularly to those who are still in service with overdue unliquidated accounts, and if warranted, withhold the salaries of the erring employees as provided under COA Circular 2012-004.

We recommended to Management the following:
the Municipal Mayor, together with the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (MDRRMC), through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO), judicially plan the utilization of the LDRRMF focusing on PPAs requiring the highest priority in terms of urgency and/or impact on the four thematic areas of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management;
the MDRRMO include in the subsequent Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) only those that can be implemented during the year through efficient planning and procurement activities; and
the MDRRMO, together with the Municipal Engineer and in cooperation with the heads of other offices concerned, expedite and ensure the full implementation of all identified priority PPAs to optimize the utilization of the 5% LDRRMF.

We recommended that Management ensure that warranty securities are required from the suppliers on procured goods, either in the form of retention money or special bank guarantee in an amount equivalent to at least one percent (1%) of the total contract price, in adherence to Section 62.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.

We recommended that Management henceforth strictly comply with the provisions of DBM Budget Circulars issued in relation to the grant and payment of Collective Negotiation Agreement (CNA) Incentive which are usually released every year. Meanwhile, we recommended that Management submit the enumerated lacking documentary requirements to corroborate the propriety and regularity of the disbursement and to justify the grant of CNA incentive.

We recommended that Management ensure strict compliance with the revised policies, rules and procedures on the preparation of the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) prescribed under Department Order (DO) No. 197, Series of 2016 and ensure proper observance of the provisions of the procurement law on conduct of pre-procurement, pre-bid and detailed evaluation of bids.

We recommended the Management to:
strictly comply with the provisions DBM and DOH Joint Circular Nos. 1 and 2, S. 2020 on the grant and payment of Special Risk Allowance (SRA) and Active Hazard Duty Pay (AHDP) to eligible Health Workers; and
direct the Municipal Accountant and the heads of other offices concerned, to submit the attendances or daily time records (DTRs) of the identified health workers for the payment of SRA under Check No. 923130 dated August 19, 2021 as well as the report on the number of COVID-19 patients attended to by the recipient Health Workers for the periods reckoning from September 15, 2020 until December 19, 2020, and December 19, 2020 to June 30, 2021.

We recommended that Management renew its Radio Station License with the National Telecommunications Commission (NTC) for the operation of fixed base radio station in the LGU in compliance with Republic Act No. 3846.

We recommended that the Liquidating Officer (LO) diligently compute all report of collections turned over by the Collectors to ascertain the accuracy of the remittances, reconcile it with the cash on hand, and record such in the cashbook. Moreover, we recommended the LO to issue an official receipt for the overage noted for its proper accounting.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain in sufficient detail its records to conveniently establish specific balances of accountabilities per account, and thereafter, the Liquidating Officer (LO) reconcile her cash book records with the accounting ledgers every end of the month or as the need arises.
Moreover, we recommended that the discrepancies identified in the cash examination report be adjusted accordingly by the LO and the Accounting Office to come up with more accurate and reliable reports.

observations.recommendation:
The Audit Team recommended that management do the following:

In view of the COVID-19 pandemic, require the inventory committee to cautiously conduct gradual/piecemeal physical count of PPE and biological assets to progressively verify actual existence, condition, and accountability of municipal assets. 

In conducting the physical count of PPE and biological assets, make use of available memorandum receipts (MR) or property acknowledgement receipts (PAR) to verify completeness of assets in each office. Moreover, for assets counted without existing PAR, such should be prepared and issued to established proper accountability. 

Include in the count all unserviceable properties as basis for disposal of assets, if warranted;

The resulting report of the physical count should be reconciled against accounting records and promptly record necessary adjustments to accurately present the value of the assets in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant cause the computation and recognition of the depreciation expense of the infrastructure assets to reflect actual carrying amounts thereof in the financial statements.

We recommended that the:

Municipal Treasurer shall, at least one month before a check becomes stale, send a written notice to the payee of the existence of the check for acceptance.

We recommended that Management ensure efficient and effective implementation of all programmed projects identified during each budget year to optimize the utilization of the fund and to warrant that goals and objectives in promoting effective ecological solid waste management are achieved.

We recommended that Management:
Revisit the Municipality’s 10-Year SWM Plan and assess the feasibility of the amounts previously projected so as not to overestimate revenues; and
Monitor the realization of the projected environmental fees to augment funds earmarked for solid waste management operations.

We recommend that the Municipality, through the office of the MENRO, comply with the recommendations of the NSWMC to facilitate the prompt approval of the LGU’s 10-Year Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan.

We recommended that management through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office:

Ensure efficient and effective implementation of all programmed projects identified during each budget year to optimize the utilization of the fund and to warrant that goals and objectives of reducing disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities are achieved.

We recommended that management judicially plan and schedule its PPAs for each budget year to expedite implementation of all planned PPAs during the year to optimize the utilization of the 20% development fund as mandated under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.

We recommended that the Management (1) ensure the full implementation of projects funded by NGAs and the timely liquidation of funds to duly settle the obligations, (2) identify the bottleneck hindering quick implementation of PPAs and adapt corrective measures, and (3) promptly return the unspent amounts after full implementation to the source agency or request from the latter an authority to use unspent funds for other related projects, if any.

We recommended that management ensure the attachment of approved DTRs, application for leave, and travel orders/ locator slips to the disbursement vouchers to support the payment of salaries and wages of the municipal employees.

We recommended that management strictly comply with the submission of the documentary requirements on the grant of RATA to municipal officials and employees. With regard the Sangguniang Bayan members, instead of submitting certificate of service rendered or approved DTR, attendances in regular sessions shall be submitted to support the RATA claims.

We recommended that management:
Refrain from granting cash advances for purposes other than what are prescribed under the law; and
Promote efficiency, economy and effectiveness on LGU operations as mandated under Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended that management ensure that the fidelity bond of accountable officers is sufficient to cover total actual cash accountability. We further recommend Management that whenever the need arises to grant cash advances to officers other than those existing Disbursing Officers, ensure that authority issued by the Head of the Agency is availed of and documented, along with the certification from the Accountant that previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books, and the duly approved application for fidelity bond adequately covering total cash advances granted.

We recommended that management regularly release barangay shares on RPT and CTC collections as prescribed under Republic Act No. 7160. We recommended that the Liquidating Officer regularly accomplish the certification of transactions at the end of each month as required under Section 63 (E) of the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

We recommended that management ensure that persons making deposits are escorted by armed guards or any capable personnel, and that transportation equipment is provided to ensure the security and safety of the accountable officer as well as the funds under his/her custody.
We recommended that the Treasury Office refrain from lending accountable forms to other agencies.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly comply with the provisions of Section 88 of the General Provisions of Republic Act No. 10924, otherwise known as the General Appropriations Act of 2017, on the submission of AAPSI report to facilitate timely validation on the actions taken by the LGU on COA findings and recommendations.

We recommended that management continuously ensure optimum implementation and delivery of Programs, Projects and Activities under the 20% development fund in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2017-1. Moreover, we recommended that management give ample attention on the construction/procurement of structures/equipment to comply with and enforce RA 9003 in order to obtain optimum result in the operation of its SLF.

The Audit Team reiterated the recommendation for management to require the immediate settlement of all long overdue cash advances.  Comply strictly with the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular. No. 97 – 002 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

observations.recommendation:
All accountable officers must continuously observe the provisions regarding issuance of accountable forms in strict numerical sequence. In addition, AOs must ensure that all pertinent information in the AFs are properly accomplished.

Management provide for adequate storage facilities to safeguard documents from loss, theft or deterioration and to effectively monitor the flow of AFs.

Management direct all AOs to solemnly abide with all relevant procedures in the requisition and issuance of Accountable Forms. Further, all AOs must immediately cease from the practice of borrowing or transferring AFs.	

Management direct all accountable officers to consume first all accountable forms still in their possession before requesting additional quantities. In particular, the Municipal Treasurer should implement rigid control procedure in the requisition and issuance of Accountable Forms.

Management designate a personnel whose task is to ensure prompt and proper preparation of the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms.

At the close of each business day, Collectors/Tellers shall turn over all their collections intact to the Local Treasurer/Cashier and prepare the corresponding Report of Collections and Deposits. An Office Order be issued to compel accountable officers of their responsibility to remit their collections on time or as soon as their collections reach P5,000.00.

The Office of the Municipal Treasurer maintain a record book of each collector for proper monitoring of their remittances.

Management direct each accountable officer to maintain an updated cashbook of his own which is separate and distinct from that of the others.

Management ensure that all transactions involving collections as well as disbursements are entered in the appropriate cashbook at the end of each day. The accountable officers must be reminded that they are entirely accountable for each cashbook under their custody and should the responsibility in the preparation of the same be delegated, adequate controls must be in place to ensure accurate entries thereon.

Both the Accounting and Treasury Offices to reconcile their accounts every month or as often as needed.

Management provide the Treasury Office with adequate vaults and other storage facilities to ensure the integrity of records and other relevant reports.

Management continuously ensure that all accountable officers have secured their respective fidelity bonds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for management to (1) stop granting cash advances to elective officials; and (2) be cognizant of the regulations governing the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances at all times.
observations.managementAction: Management did not allow cash advances of other than TEV to elective officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for management to (1) analyze and reconcile each the CO, MOOE component of the Disaster Fund and compensate from one what is due to the other with consideration of legislative action, where legally necessary; and (2) abide strictly the charging of expenses with due consideration of the underlying authority to disburse LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Compensated the amount erroneously charged with that of the amount for transfer to the Trust Fund - LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit the mandatory reports required by regulation relative to the use of government vehicles and the consumption of gasoline.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented the submission of the mandatory reports required by regulation relative to the use of government vehicles and the consumption of gasoline.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit to the audit team all required documents relevant to the purchased lot as provided in the aforementioned regulation.
observations.managementAction: Management submited to the audit team all required documents

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality initiate activities to pursue the formulation and operationalization of its Gender and Development (GAD) Code.
observations.managementAction: The legislative is on the second reading of the proposed GAD Code

observations.recommendation: We recommended for management to (1) immediately draw the replacement of staled check amounting to P40,031.23 and deposit the same to the National Treasury; (2) coordinate from sources of the PDAF or to the national treasury in obtaining the account required by the latter; and (3) exert diligent effort to identify the sources of the PDAF amounting to P53,661.00 and immediately revert the same to the National Treasury.
observations.managementAction: Management took action  the return of the PDAF balance but was unable to determine the correct account to facilitate the deposit to the BTr.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 13, Page 56, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management grant advance payments only for contract of services on (a) Hotel and restaurants; (b) use of conference/seminars and exhibit areas; and (c) lease of office space but not for photo coverage documentation.
observations.managementAction: Management no longer grant cash advance on similar transaction

observations.recommendation: We recommended for management to (1) strictly comply with the documentary requirements in the payment of overtime services; and (2) submit immediately the documents needed to support the transaction under review.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the documentary requirements on similar transaction.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should settle immediately the disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Continuous collection from responsible persons is being undertaken by management.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 28, Page 76 Part II of this AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended for management to immediately reconcile its records and make the necessary adjustments on the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management adjusted and provided reciprocal account as audited but the balance of the FS as of 2016 remained not reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to strictly adhere to the regulations in the use of the Special Education Fund.
observations.managementAction: No action taken
observations.reason: Management strictly adhered to the regulations in the use of the Special Education Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for management to properly identify major PPAs where GAD activities were integrated so as to properly determine and quantify the extent, effectiveness, and efficiency of the LGU in addressing gender issues.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to properly identify the streamlined GAD activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management include all procurement activities that it plans to undertake within the calendar year in the APP and ensure that all pertinent information/data as provided under Sec. 3.2 GPPB Circular 1-2009 and Sec. 7.3 of the IRR of RA 9184 are included therein.
observations.managementAction: Management included all procurement activities that it plans to undertake within the calendar year in the APP

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should strictly comply with the provisions of Section 53(e) of IRR-A, RA 9184, or the Government Procurement Reform Act by way of requisitioning their needed office supplies from the Procurement Service under the DBM
observations.managementAction: Partially undertook procurement at DBM PS.
observations.reason: The agency did not implement the audit recommendation due to the following: 1.  Accessibility – Suppliers in the locality are more readily accessible. It is more convenient to return defective supplies. DBM PS is too far and inconvenient to the procuring entity. 2.  Availability – All supplies requested by the end– users in the LGU are available anytime with suppliers. 3.  Quality – The quality of supplies available to the suppliers are above par. 4. Economic Upliftment in the Province - Patronizing the local suppliers will redound to economic boost and prosperity in the province

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Treasurer should locate the missing vouchers and submit to the Accounting Office for recording in the books. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should draw Journal Entry Voucher to take up all valid reconciling items disclosed in the bank reconciliation statements and record such adjustments in the General Ledger.
observations.managementAction: Partially identified reconciling items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management should effect the immediate reclassification of the costs of obsolete and unserviceable items to the account Other Assets so that the true value of the affected account shall be reflected in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Adjustment of the account was undertaken. Subject equipment were subsequently sold

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management implement the Supplemental Feeding Program without unreasonable delay and to cause the immediate liquidation of the fund in order not to hamper the release of the succeeding funds, thus ensuring the sustainability of the program.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to implement the program within the time set by regulation and comply with the liquidation requirement.
